text:
There was once a young rabbit who had an extraordinary talent for remembering everything. Every leaf it touched would never fade. One autumn day, as the leaves began to golden-yellow, the young rabbit began a grand journey. It wanted to witness every leaf turn.

As it trotted through the forest, it never forgot a sight—it would revisit every place it had ever seen a leaf turn. Birds singing at dawn, a doe with antlers shining in the light, even that time it saw a squirrel reading a book. Every detail.

One evening, as the moon rose high into the sky, a wise old owl hooted from a branch.

"What are you doing all night?" the owl asked.

"I'm memorizing everything," the young rabbit told it.

For months, the rabbit would spend nights flying over the forest, catching glints of gold in the dim light. Each leaf became locked into its mind. Flowers whispered secrets about the nest's nestlings. Even stones told of distant times.
